blurb:
Sebastian is an adult male domestic shorthair at Good Mews, and his photo gives off very strong "I have opinions about where you sit" energy. He hasn't had a full bio written yet, so his full story is still waiting to be told, but adult male cats like Sebastian tend to come with a personality already baked in. What you see is what you get, which a lot of people prefer over the chaos of a kitten.

Adult cats also tend to get overlooked because everyone gravitates toward the young ones. Sebastian has probably been waiting a little longer than he should have. If you want a cat who's already grown into himself and doesn't need much figuring out, that's worth considering.

Good Mews is a no-kill, cage-free shelter on Robinson Road in Marietta. Reach out by phone or email to set up a time to meet him.
shelter_hours: Please contact shelter for current hours
scoring_notes:
• Adult male cats are statistically the hardest to place — his feature directly addresses an adoption gap
• Blurb explicitly signals he's been overlooked longer than he deserves, creating reader empathy
• "Already grown into himself" framing is a strong sell for readers who want predictable, low-drama companionship

blurb:
Eugene is an adult domestic shorthair who doesn't have a full bio written yet, but one look at him tells you there's a lot going on behind those eyes. He's got the kind of face that makes you want to know his story, and whatever it is, he's clearly landed somewhere safe now.

His profile is still being built out by the rescue, so the details are thin for now. What we do know: he's a grown cat, which means he's past the chaos of kittenhood and ready to actually be a companion. Adult cats tend to get overlooked for kittens, which is honestly a shame. They know who they are. They settle in, they find their spot on the couch, and they stick with you.

If you're curious about Eugene and want to know more before the rescue finishes his profile, reach out directly to Three Paws Rescue through their Petfinder page. They'll be able to tell you what he's like in person, which is the only introduction that really counts anyway.
scoring_notes:
• Adult shorthair with no completed bio limits reader connection and adoption urgency
• Backstory is entirely speculative — "a lot going on behind those eyes" offers no real hook
• No time-at-shelter signals; incomplete profile suggests recent intake, not a long wait
